title: Block and Unblock access to CloudFront distribution using AWS WAF

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top. Click on S3 in the Storage section.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top. Click on S3 in the Storage section.
Click on the S3 bucket name starting with lab to open.
Click on the S3 bucket name starting with lab to open.
This bucket is created during the start of project, Click on the S3 object "WAF_CF_template.template" to open.
This bucket is created during the start of project, Click on the S3 object "WAF_CF_template.template" to open.
To copy the Object URL, click on the copy icon of the Object URL.
To copy the Object URL, click on the copy icon of the Object URL.
Copy and save it to the notepad, you will need this during the CloudFormation Stack creation.
Copied content example: https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Copy and save it to the notepad, you will need this during the CloudFormation Stack creation.
Copied content example: https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Navigate to CloudFormation. Click Services, click on CloudFormation in the Management & Governance section.
Navigate to CloudFormation. Click Services, click on CloudFormation in the Management & Governance section.
On the CloudFormation dashboard, click on Create Stack
On the CloudFormation dashboard, click on Create Stack
For Step-1, Specify template.
Prerequisite - Prepare template : Select Choose an existing template
Specify Template :
Template source : Select Amazon S3 URL
Amazon S3 URL : Paste the URL copied from earlier. https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Click on Next.
For Step-1, Specify template.
Prerequisite - Prepare template : Select Choose an existing template
Prerequisite - Prepare template : Select Choose an existing template
Specify Template :
Template source : Select Amazon S3 URL
Amazon S3 URL : Paste the URL copied from earlier. https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Click on Next.
Specify Template :
Template source : Select Amazon S3 URL
Template source : Select Amazon S3 URL
Amazon S3 URL : Paste the URL copied from earlier. https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Amazon S3 URL : Paste the URL copied from earlier. https://s3.amazonaws.com/lab.1379.27205996/WAF_CF_template.template
Click on Next.
For Step-2, Specify stack details
Stack Name: Enter DemoStack
Keep all the options as default
Click on Next button.
For Step-2, Specify stack details
Stack Name: Enter DemoStack
Stack Name: Enter DemoStack
Keep all the options as default
Keep all the options as default
Click on Next button.
Click on Next button.
For Step-3, Configure stack options,
In the Permissions tab, select the IAM role name. In the dropdown, please select the IAM role.
Keep remaining everything as default and click on Next button.
For Step-3, Configure stack options,
In the Permissions tab, select the IAM role name. In the dropdown, please select the IAM role.
In the Permissions tab, select the IAM role name. In the dropdown, please select the IAM role.
Keep remaining everything as default and click on Next button.
Stack creation is having status as CREATE_IN_PROGRESS and it may take up to 15 minutes for the status to change to CREATE_COMPLETE.
Stack creation is having status as CREATE_IN_PROGRESS and it may take up to 15 minutes for the status to change to CREATE_COMPLETE.
After some time, the status has changed to CREATE_COMPLETE.
After some time, the status has changed to CREATE_COMPLETE.
Switch to the Outputs tab and click to open WebsiteURL and CloudFront URL in a new tab.
Switch to the Outputs tab and click to open WebsiteURL and CloudFront URL in a new tab.
Click On Services And Select WAF & Shield Under The Security, Identity And Compliance Section.
In the left panel, scroll to the bottom and click Switch to the old WAF Console.
Open IP Sets section from left sidebar and perform the following task:
• From drop down make sure it is Global (CloudFront).
• Click on the Create IP Set button for creating IPv4 IP set
• Name: Enter IP_match_v4
• Select IP version: IPv4
Open IP Sets section from left sidebar and perform the following task:
• From drop down make sure it is Global (CloudFront).
• Click on the Create IP Set button for creating IPv4 IP set
• Name: Enter IP_match_v4
• Select IP version: IPv4
IP addresses : 10.0.0.0/32
IP addresses : 10.0.0.0/32
• Keep all the fields as default and click on the Create IP Set button.
• IP_match_v4 IP Set is created successfully.
• From drop down make sure it is Global (CloudFront).
• Click on the Create IP Set button again for creating ipv6 IP set
• Name: Enter IP_match_v6
• Select IP version as IPv6
Add IP address : 2001:db8:a0b:12f0::1/128
• Keep all the fields as default and click on the Create IP Set button.
• IP_match_v6 IP Set is created successfully.
Open Web ACLs from left menu
Change the Filter to Global (CloudFront).
Click on the Create web ACL button.
For Step-1, Name web ACL, fill the below details:
• Resource Type: Select Global resources (CloudFront Distributions and AWS Amplify Applications)
• Web ACL Name: Enter Whiz
• CloudWatch metric name: Enter Whiz
• Under Associate Click on Add AWS Resources button .
• A pop up will appear Add AWS resources to associate: Select the CloudFront distribution present as shown below.
• Click Add button
• Click on the Next button to proceed.
For Step-2, Add Rules and rule groups, complete the below requirements given:
• Click on the Add rule button then select Add my own rules and rule groups.
• Select Rule builder.
• Name: Enter Rule
• Rule type: Regular rule (default)
• To Add Statement and action, Choose the following:
• if a request : matches the statement
• inspect : Originate from an IP address in
• IP Set : IP_match_v4.
• Action : Block
• Click on the Add Rule button.
• Click on the Add rule button again then select Add my own rules and rule groups.
• Select Rule builder.
• Name: Enter Rule2
• Rule type: Regular rule (default)
• To Add Statement and action, Choose the following:
• if a request : matches the statement
• inspect : Originate from an IP address in
• IP Set : IP_match_v6.
• Action : Block
For Step-2, Add Rules and rule groups, complete the below requirements given:
• Click on the Add rule button then select Add my own rules and rule groups.
• Select Rule builder.
• Name: Enter Rule
• Rule type: Regular rule (default)
• To Add Statement and action, Choose the following:
• if a request : matches the statement
• inspect : Originate from an IP address in
• IP Set : IP_match_v4.
• Action : Block
• Click on the Add Rule button.
• Click on the Add rule button again then select Add my own rules and rule groups.
• Select Rule builder.
• Name: Enter Rule2
• Rule type: Regular rule (default)
• To Add Statement and action, Choose the following:
• if a request : matches the statement
• inspect : Originate from an IP address in
• IP Set : IP_match_v6.
• Action : Block
• Click on the Add Rule button.
• Default Web ACL action for requests that don't match any rules: Select Allow.
• Click on the Next button.
For Step-3, keep Set rule priority as default and click Next.
For Step-4, keep Configure metrics as default and click Next.
For Step-5, review and click on Create Web ACL button.
Web ACL Whiz is created successfully.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Click on the to Instances on the left panel.
Click on the to Instances on the left panel.
Select the instance by clicking on the Instance id.
Select the instance by clicking on the Instance id.
Click on the Connect button.
Click on the Connect button.
Switch to the Session Manager tab and click on the Connect button.
Switch to the Session Manager tab and click on the Connect button.
The session manager will start the session that can be used for SSH.
The session manager will start the session that can be used for SSH.
Change the directory to root:
Change the directory to root:
Check the present work directory:
Make sure both the files are present, run the below command to list the files present in this directory:
Run the below command for blocking the EC2 Instance website:
Run the below command for blocking the EC2 Instance website:
Refresh both CloudFront and Website tab.
CloudFront's distribution domain will work fine but the Website one will not open because the script has blocked and restricted access for EC2 and only allowed from CloudFront's distribution domain.
Block the access of CloudFront also. Paste the below command to the session manager's terminal:
Refresh the CloudFront distribution page.
Note: It may take up to 10 minutes for the changes to be reflected in CloudFront. Please refresh after every 30 seconds if the above error is not appearing.
Click On Services And Select WAF & Shield Under The Security, Identity And Compliance Section.
Click On Services And Select WAF & Shield Under The Security, Identity And Compliance Section.
Go to Web ACLs click on the Rule button, first you need to delete both rules then you can delete IP sets because it is attached to the Web ACLs.
Go to Web ACLs click on the Rule button, first you need to delete both rules then you can delete IP sets because it is attached to the Web ACLs.
Navigate To IP Sets From Left Sidebar, select both IP one by one.
Navigate To IP Sets From Left Sidebar, select both IP one by one.
Change The Filter To Global(Cloudfront)
Change The Filter To Global(Cloudfront)
Click On The IP_match_v4, now click on the Delete button.
Click On The IP_match_v4, now click on the Delete button.
Same way select IP_match_v6 And Delete By Clicking On The Delete Button.
Same way select IP_match_v6 And Delete By Clicking On The Delete Button.
IP's Are Deleted When You See The Confirmation.
IP's Are Deleted When You See The Confirmation.
Run the below command.
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
--group-id $(aws ec2 describe-instances --instance-ids $(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id) --query 'Reservations[0].Instances[0].SecurityGroups[0].GroupId' --output text) \
--protocol tcp \
--port 80 \
Now Refresh The CloudFront Distribution Domain Page and Website URL Page.
Run the below command.
Now Refresh The CloudFront Distribution Domain Page and Website URL Page.
Note: It may take up to 1 minute for the page to be ready.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :

Navigate to CloudFormation by Clicking on Services, click on CloudFormation in the Management & Governance section.
Click on the Stacks present on the left sidebar.
Click on the Stacks present on the left sidebar.
Select the CloudFormation stack present and Click on the Delete button.
Select the CloudFormation stack present and Click on the Delete button.
Confirm the deletion by clicking on the Delete stack button.
Confirm the deletion by clicking on the Delete stack button.

CloudFormation stack is deleted successfully.

You have successfully created and launched Amazon EC2 Instance using the CloudFormation stack.

You have successfully created CloudFront distribution using the CloudFormation stack.

You have successfully created Web ACL using AWS WAF Classic.

You have successfully done SSH using Session Manager.

You have successfully blocked traffic of the EC2 instance's website and CloudFront distribution domain by running the scripts.

You have successfully updated the Web ACL for allowing CloudFront distribution domain.
Sign out of AWS Account.
Sign out of AWS Account.

You have successfully completed the project.

Once you have completed the steps, click on End Project from the lab console.
